tips.watering: Water once a week. It is best to water when the top 2 inches of soil feel dry.
tips.sunlight: Thrives in bright, indirect light. Direct sunlight can scorch the leaves.
tips.fertilizing: Apply a liquid fertilizer once a month during spring and summer.

tips.watering: Keep the topsoil lightly moist. Avoid letting water sit at the bottom of the pot.
tips.sunlight: Prefers bright to medium indirect light.
tips.fertilizing: Feed with a balanced indoor plant fertilizer every 6-8 weeks.

tips.watering: Needs very little water. Water only when the soil has dried out completely.
tips.sunlight: Can survive in low light, but thrives in bright, indirect light.
tips.fertilizing: Fertilizing twice a year (during spring and autumn) is sufficient.

tips.watering: Keep the soil consistently moist. If leaves start drooping, it needs water.
tips.fertilizing: Use a bloom-booster fertilizer once a month during the flowering season.

tips.watering: Allow the top inch of soil to dry out between waterings.
tips.fertilizing: Fertilize during the growing season once a month.

tips.watering: Water deeply only when the soil is completely dry.
tips.sunlight: Requires at least 6 hours of bright sunlight daily.
tips.fertilizing: Use a specialized succulent fertilizer once a month during summer.
